title: Add group label to Kanban cards
excerpt: Currently, we’re able to colour code Kanban cards, and we’re able to customize cards to show certain fields (status, date, etc). We’re also able to split out the Kanban by groups. What I cannot seem to do, however, is ad…

posts.author: vagen (Valentin Agen)
posts.cookedHtml: Currently, we’re able to colour code Kanban cards, and we’re able to customize cards to show certain fields (status, date, etc). We’re also able to split out the Kanban by groups. What I cannot seem to do, however, is add a field on the Kanban cards that shows the group it belongs to. I use card colour coding for something else already, and I don’t want to split the whole board vertically by group either. It would be nice to have a neat little label showing which group the Kanban card belongs to, similar to how fields can be added as labels on the Kanban card. Ideally, with the same square colour icon beside it and the “group” label matching the group colour.

posts.author: cwatson (Connor Watson)
posts.cookedHtml:
Could not agree more.

I use a Monday board to track POs, and I have a Kanban view that separates open and closed POs. It shows the PO numberm but I have a different group for each type of part.

posts.author: kranthi_thoughtflow (Kranthi Kiran)
posts.cookedHtml:
image1542×726 167 KB

You should be able to do this on our app Multi Board Kanban
In the “Customize Card Fields” section on the right, you should see some additional fields to display on the cards. You can select ‘Group” here to see it on the card.

If you use Kanban regularly and for multiple projects, I would say this is a must have for your team.
